heroSubtitle: RESEARCH, PROTOTYPES, AND TEAM INITIATIVES BEYOND THE CAR

projects.title: Development of BMS
projects.imageAlt: In-house Battery Management System circuit and testing interface

sections.heading: Project Purpose & Objectives
sections.text: Developed a high-performance in-house BMS featuring adaptive balancing and predictive fault detection to enhance battery longevity, safety, and reliability under real-world operating conditions.

sections.heading: Architectural Implementation
sections.text: Implemented a modular Master-Slave topology for scalable deployment, enabling high-precision cell monitoring, reduced wiring complexity, and seamless integration across applications.

sections.heading: Testing & Diagnostic Validation
sections.text: Incorporated advanced diagnostic algorithms with rigorous stress, load, and thermal testing to ensure accurate fault detection and stable operation under dynamic conditions.

sections.heading: Strategic Value & IP Potential
sections.text: Focuses on a cost-effective, innovation-driven design with strong potential for patentable diagnostic and safety IP, bridging research and deployable industrial solutions.

projects.title: Development of Motor-Controller
projects.imageAlt: High-efficiency motor controller PCB and diagnostic interface

sections.heading: Project Purpose & Objectives
sections.text: Developed a high-efficiency PMSM controller using Field-Oriented Control (FOC) for smooth torque/speed and fast dynamic response.

sections.heading: Architectural Implementation
sections.text: Uses high-speed current sensing and Space Vector Pulse Width Modulation (SVPWM) for precise control with reduced speed ripples and inverter noises.

sections.heading: Testing & Diagnostic Validation
sections.text: PID-tuned current and speed control loops with validation under transients and various running conditions.

sections.heading: Strategic Value & IP Potential
sections.text: Focus on efficiency and thermal optimization, enabling reliable, patentable motor control intellectual property.

projects.title: Development of VCU
projects.imageAlt: Vehicle Control Unit showing centralized communication and safety interlocks

sections.heading: Project Purpose & Objectives
sections.text: Developed a centralized VCU to act as the primary intelligence hub, coordinating communication between the BMS, motor controller, and sensors.

sections.heading: Architectural Implementation
sections.text: Integrates safety interlock logic with multi-protocol communication (e.g., CAN) to synchronize real-time data, managing seamless interaction between sensors, control units, and driver-facing systems.

sections.heading: Testing & Diagnostic Validation
sections.text: Utilizes Hardware-in-the-Loop (HIL) testing and edge-case simulations to validate safety mechanisms, ensuring robust handling of faults, communication delays, and subsystem failures.

sections.heading: Strategic Value & IP Potential
sections.text: Focuses on a modular, scalable software stack with proprietary energy management and fault-handling strategies, enabling advanced diagnostics and strong potential for patentable vehicle control IP.

projects.title: Development of H-CBM Vehicle
projects.imageAlt: Hydrogen-enriched CNG engine test bench and emission monitoring setup

sections.heading: Overview
sections.text: Utilizes a 5% H2 and 95% Methane mixture to improve the thermal efficiency of the engine and optimize the combustion process.

sections.heading: Project Purpose & Objectives
sections.text: Develop a hydrogen-enriched CNG powertrain to significantly reduce CO, THC, and NOx emissions while maintaining engine performance.

sections.heading: Architectural Implementation
sections.text: Features a recalibrated ECU, specialized fuel delivery system, H2-compatible storage tanks, and a safety-certified retrofit kit compatible with existing CNG engines.

sections.heading: Testing & Diagnostic Validation
sections.text: During emissions testing, a significant decrease in CO2 emissions relative to petrol and standard CNG was verified under varying load conditions.

sections.heading: Strategic Value & IP Potential
sections.text: Provides a scalable, cost-effective transition to cleaner mobility by leveraging existing CNG infrastructure, offering high potential for proprietary retrofit technology.

projects.title: Passive Aerodynamic System
projects.imageAlt: Passive aerodynamic elements including front wheel diffuser and rear wing

sections.heading: Overview
sections.text: Development of a passive aerodynamic system designed to enhance vehicle stability and cornering performance without the use of active control mechanisms.

sections.heading: Key Components
sections.text: The system integrates a front wheel diffuser for managing turbulent airflow around rotating wheels and a rear wing for generating consistent downforce. These components work together to improve airflow attachment and reduce instability.

sections.heading: Design Approach
sections.text: Focused on passive flow control techniques to guide air efficiently across the vehicle body. Special attention was given to minimizing aerodynamic losses caused by wheel wake and optimizing pressure distribution across the rear section.

sections.heading: Performance Impact
sections.text: The system aims to improve cornering grip by increasing downforce and stabilizing airflow, resulting in better handling characteristics and higher confidence during high-speed turns.

projects.title: MREX-03 Key Insights
projects.imageAlt: MREX-03 vehicle chassis and modular component layout

sections.heading: Overview
sections.text: A comprehensive review of the MREX-03 development cycle, focusing on engineering refinement and manufacturing excellence.

sections.heading: Key Insights
sections.text: Emphasized the importance of Design for Manufacturing (DFM) and realistic tolerance planning. Highlighted the critical need for early system-level validation and safety margin analysis to prevent integration bottlenecks.

sections.heading: Design Philosophy
sections.text: Prioritized modular and serviceable designs, which proved essential for rapid iteration during testing and improved overall vehicle reliability.

sections.heading: Targets for 2027
sections.text: Aims to clear all technical inspections and achieve top-tier performance in dynamic events. The primary goal is to deliver a fully furbished, modular E-Race car platform to serve as the foundation for future development cycles.
